17.gl: properly, something inherited, i.e. (abstractly) occupancy, or (concretely) an heirloom; generally an estate, patrimony or portion
17.kj: heritage, to inherit, inheritance, possession. Compare H5158 (נַחַל).

18.gl: the earth (at large, or partitively a land)
18.kj: [idiom] common, country, earth, field, ground, land, [idiom] natins, way, [phrase] wilderness, world.
18.dv: from an unused root probably meaning to be firm;

18.gl: a people (as a congregated unit); specifically, a tribe (as those of Israel); hence (collectively) troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock
to be (causatively, make) zealous, i.e. (in a bad sense) jealous or envious
18.kj: (be) envy(-ious), be (move to, provoke to) jealous(-y), [idiom] very, (be) zeal(-ous).

18.gl: Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
18.kj: Jehovah, the Lord. Compare H3050 (יָהּ), H3069 (יְהֹוִה).

18.gl: to commiserate; by implication, to spare
